rulings.Case Title: ALVA RANSFORD VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Application To Appear As Counsel Pro Hac Vice; Verified Application; And Memorandum Of Points And Authorities To Appear As Counsel Pro Hac Vice
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, FEBRUARY 20, 2008 LINE 20. DEFENDANT GRIFFIN WHEEL COMPANY, INC.'S Motion And Application OF LARRY D. OTTOWAY, ESQUIRE AND AMY SHERRY FISCHER, ESQUIRE To Appear As Counsel Pro Hac Vice IS GRANTED, NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RENNA HOLMES
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, FEBRUARY 20, 2008 LINE 1. PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE APPEAL OF THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION IS NOT A BASIS FOR DEFERRING A RULING ON THIS MOTION FOR MR. CHOYCE IS NOT INVOLVED IN THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE COMPLAINT, PARAGRAPHS SIX, TEN, TWELVE AND FOURTEEN, STATE A MALPRACTICE CLAIM, NOT A FRAUD CLAIM. THE ALLEGATIONS OF FRAUD ARE CONTRADICTED BY ALLEGATIONS THAT THE WILL WAS READ TO THE DECEDENT. THE COMPLAINT FAILS TO ALLEGE A DUTY OF CARE BECAUSE COLVIN WAS NOT AN INTENDED BENEFICIARY AND NO FACTS ARE ALLEGED THAT ESTABLISH A FIDUCIARY RELATIONSHIP BEWTWEEN COLVIN AND CHOYCE. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: DAN STACKHOUSE VS. MACY'S WEST, INC. DBA FEDERATED DEPARTMENT STORES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Macy'S Department Stores, Inc., Mike Monroe, Macy'S West, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion To 1)Stay The Action While This Motion Is Pending; 2)Compel Arbitration And 3) Dismiss The Action Or, The Alternatve, Stay The Action Pending Arbitration; Request For Judicial Notice; Appendix Of Out Of State Authorities.
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, FEBRUARY 20, 2008 LINE 15. DEFENDANTS MACY'S DEPARTMENT STORES, INC., MIKE MONROE AND MACY'S WEST'S MOTION (1) STAY THE ACTION WHILE THIS MOTION IS PENDING, (2) COMPEL ARBITRATION, AND (3) DISMISS THE ACTION, OR, THE ALTERNATIVE, STAY THE ACTION PENDING ARBITRATION; REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE: THE MOTION IS GRANTED, THE ACTION IS STAYED PENDING THE ARBITRATION. THE EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT PLAINTIFF WAS APPRISED OF HIS RIGHT TO OPT OUT OF THE ARBITRATION PROGRAM AND ON TWO OCCASIONS AND DID NOT DO SO. THE PROGRAM IS NEITHER PROCEDURALLY NOR SUBSTANTIVELY UNCONSCIONABLE. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE ARE OVERRULED BUT FOR THE PHRASE "IN PRACTICE" IN PARAGRAPH FIFTEEN OF THE GLICK DECLARATION. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE OVERRULED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JACK A. NISSIM VS. CAL STATE 9 CREDIT UNION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, FEBRUARY 20, 2008 LINE 8. DEFENDANT WORLD SAVINGS BANK, F.S.B.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED AS TO FIRST, SECOND AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION, TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE STATEMENTS MADE BY WORLD OR CREDIT UNION TO PLAINTIFF AND FURTHER ALLEGES THAT DUDUM AND ATLANTIC WERE ACTING FOR PLAINTIFF. AS A CONSEQUENCE, THEIR STATEMENTS ARE NOT BINDING ON WORLD OR CREDIT UNION. SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THIRD AND FIFTH CAUSES OF ACTION. LENDERS DO NOT OWE A BORROWER A DUTY OF CARE ABSENT PLEADING SPECIAL CIRCUMSTANCES AND NONE ARE ALLEGED. OVERRULED AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION BASED ON ALLEGATIONS OF MUTUAL MISTAKE. =(302/PJM/AY)
